text:
The "Watcher" Value Proposition
You nailed it. Here's the key insight:
SYSTEM UNDER STRESS
mongod is slow/unresponsive
— serverStatus takes 500ms+ (mongotune detects this)
- mongod's own FTDC may be delayed or incomplete
mongod logs may be delayed
mongotune (separate process) continues recording:
- "serverStatus took 2000ms" (its own measurement)
- "FTDC collection was stalled" (self-awareness )
- CPU pressure from /proc (independent of mongod)
— Memory pressure from /proc (independent of mongod)
- Its own timestamps showing the degradation timeline
When mongod is dying:
- mongod's FTDC may have gaps or delays
- mongod's logs may be buffered and lost on crash
- mongod can't tell you how slow it was to respond
Mongotune captures:
- External observation of mongod's latency (status_cmd_latency_millis)
- System-level signals (CPU/memory from /proc, not
from mongod)
- Its own stall detection ("I tried to collect at T, but didn't run until T+5s")
- Complete timeline even if mongod crashes
---
Summary: What Mongotune Adds to Monitoring
Feature
FTDC
Latency measurement
System metrics
Crash survival
Stall detection
mongod alone
mongod's internal view
Self-reported
Some via serverStatus
Lost if mongod dies
Limited
Rate limiting visibility
Server-side counters
With mongotune
+ external observer's view
External measurement of response time
Direct from /proc (independent)
Mongotune's data survives
Detects its own stalls
+ policy decision history
